At the highest point of the image, about thirty yards high, there likely was a pedestal, and it probably was covered only with plates of gold, not a solid mass of that precious metal. Pride and prejudice cause people to require their subjects to follow their religion, whether right or wrong, and when worldly interests attract them, and punishment intimidates them, few refuse. This is easy for the careless, sensual, and unfaithful, who are the majority; and most will go their own ways. There is nothing so bad that the careless world will not be drawn to by music, or driven to by a fiery furnace. By such methods, false worship has been established and maintained.

True devotion calms the spirit, quiets it, and softens it, but superstition and devotion to false gods inflame people's passions. The matter is put into a small space: Burn, or turn. Proud people are still ready to say, as Nebuchadnezzar did, Who is the Lord that I should fear his power? Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego did not hesitate whether they should comply or not. Life or death were not to be considered. Those who want to avoid sin must not negotiate with temptation when what attracts or frightens them is clearly evil. Do not pause about it, but say, as Christ did, Go away from me, Satan. They did not devise an evasive answer when a direct answer was expected. Those who make their duty their main concern need not be anxious or fearful concerning the outcome. The faithful servants of God find him able to control and overrule all powers armed against them. Lord, if you will, you can. If He is for us, we need not fear what man can do to us. God will deliver us, either from death or in death. They must obey God rather than man; they must rather suffer than sin; and must not do evil that good may come. Therefore none of these things moved them. Saving them from sinful compliance was as great a miracle in the kingdom of grace as saving them out of the fiery furnace was in the kingdom of nature. Fear of people and love of the world, especially lack of faith, make people yield to temptation, while a firm belief in the truth will deliver them from denying Christ or being ashamed of him. We are to be gentle in our replies, but we must be decided that we will obey God rather than man.

Let Nebuchadnezzar heat his furnace as hot as he can; a few minutes will end the torment of those cast into it; but hellfire tortures and yet does not kill. Those who worshipped the beast and its image have no rest, no pause, no moment free from pain. Now was fulfilled in detail that great promise: When you walk through the fire, you will not be burned. Leaving it to God who preserved them in the fire to bring them out, they walked up and down in the midst, supported and encouraged by the presence of the Son of God. Those who suffer for Christ have his presence in their sufferings, even in the fiery furnace and in the valley of the shadow of death. Nebuchadnezzar acknowledges them as servants of the most high God; a God able to deliver them out of his hand. It is our God only who is the consuming fire. If we could but see into the eternal world, we should behold the persecuted believer safe from the malice of his foes, while they are exposed to the wrath of God and tormented in unquenchable fires.

What God did for these his servants would help keep the Jews to their religion while in captivity and cure them of idolatry. The miracle brought deep convictions on Nebuchadnezzar. But no lasting change then took place in his conduct. He who preserved these pious Jews in the fiery furnace is able to uphold us in the hour of temptation and keep us from falling into sin.
